content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a tricky game with a sequence a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​. Initially, his score is S=1S = 1S=1. In the game, he performs the following process exactly KKK times (note that the sequence aaa never changes):


Pick an element XXX from the sequence aaa uniformly randomly.
Change the score SSS to (S⋅X)%N(S \cdot X) \% N(S⋅X)%N.
Find the expected value of Chef's score SSS at the end of the game. It can be shown that this expected value can be expressed as a fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers, P≥0P \ge 0P≥0, Q>0Q \gt 0Q>0 and QQQ is coprime with 109+710^9 + 7109+7. You should compute P⋅Q−1%(109+7)P \cdot Q^{-1} \% (10^9 + 7)P⋅Q−1%(109+7), where Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 denotes the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: 0≤ai<N0 \le a_i \lt N0≤ai​<N for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Regardless of Chef's exact choices, the final score is always (1⋅1⋅1)%2=1(1 \cdot 1 \cdot 1) \% 2 = 1(1⋅1⋅1)%2=1.
